seo.title: Surroundings | ArdenNest — Walks, cycling, adventure and attractions
seo.description: Discover the best spots around ArdenNest. Walking routes, cycling paths, adventure parks for kids and teens, restaurants and attractions in the Belgian Ardennes.

title: The Surroundings
h1: Walks, cycling routes and attractions near ArdenNest
subtitle: Discover the most beautiful spots around ArdenNest
intro: Although most guests prefer to stay on the property - the garden is big enough after all - there is plenty to experience in the area. Below you'll find our personal recommendations.
openInMaps: View your route from the holiday home

walks.title: Walks
walks.description: Our favorite walks nearby, tested by us and our guests. From quiet village walks to adventurous train walks.
walks.fromPropertyDescription: Put on your hiking boots and start at the front door. No car needed.
walks.nearbyDescription: A bit further away, but absolutely worth it. Reachable by car or train.
walks.walkingMapsNote: Detailed walking maps with instructions are available in the house, in Dutch, French, English and German.

cycling.title: Cycling
cycling.description: From flat RAVeL paths along the river to challenging mountain bike routes through the forests.

active.title: Active & Adventure
active.description: Climb, fly and scramble — the Ardennes are one big playground for kids and teens.

exclusive.title: Exclusive for guests
exclusive.description: Experiences you can't book anywhere else — exclusively for ArdenNest guests.

attractions.title: Attractions
attractions.description: The Ardennes have a lot to offer. These destinations are perfect for a day trip.

restaurants.title: Restaurants & takeaway
restaurants.description: Our favourite places to eat nearby. From cozy brasseries to pizza and fresh fries.

shops.title: Shops
shops.description: Everything you need for a carefree stay. By shopping here, you're supporting local businesses – and discovering the authentic flavors of the region.

note: Most groups prefer to stay on the property - the garden is big enough for days of fun. But if you want to go out, we're ready with tips!

treinwandeling-anseremme-gendron.title: Train Walk Anseremme - Gendron - Anseremme
treinwandeling-anseremme-gendron.description: Our favorite family walk! Take the train from Dinant-Anseremme to Gendron and walk back along the beautiful Lesse river. Suitable for buggies and perfect for a day out.
treinwandeling-anseremme-gendron.buggyNote: Lift buggy 3x at stairs/bridges, otherwise smooth paved path
treinwandeling-anseremme-gendron.tip: Too early for the train? Walk across the Dinant bridge and wait on a bench along the Meuse - lovely in good weather!
treinwandeling-anseremme-gendron.fullDescription:
This is undoubtedly our favorite walk in the area and perfect for families with children of all ages.

## How does it work?

You take the train at Dinant-Anseremme station (not the main station!) towards Bertrix. After just 5 minutes you get off in Gendron. The weekend fare is only €2.50 per person - a bargain!

From Gendron station you walk back to Dinant, following the Lesse river the whole way. The route is about 8 km and takes around 3 hours, including breaks.

## Highlights along the way

The walk offers beautiful variety:

- **The high railway bridge** - Halfway through you cross twice via an impressive old railway bridge, high above the Lesse. Perfectly safe with railings, but spectacular!
- **Walzin beach** - A lovely spot to pause. In warm weather the children can paddle in the water. Ideal for a picnic.
- **Walzin Castle** - The fairytale castle on the rocks is one of the most beautiful views of the walk.
- **Pedestrian bridge** - Just before Dinant you cross via a narrow pedestrian bridge.

## With a buggy?

This walk is largely buggy-friendly! The path is paved and flat. Only at the two railway bridges and the pedestrian bridge do you need to lift the buggy briefly (a few steps each time). No climbing or scrambling.

## Arrival in Dinant

You arrive along the Meuse with a beautiful view of the citadel and the cathedral of Dinant. Walk on to the supermarket for a well-deserved ice cream!
routeSteps: Go to Dinant-Anseremme station (not the main station)
Take the train towards Bertrix (5 min, €2.50 weekend)
Follow the walking path along the Lesse towards Dinant
Cross via the high railway bridge (2x)
Admire Walzin Castle on the rocks
Arrival along the Meuse in Dinant

treinwandeling-houyet-gendron.title: Train Walk Houyet - Gendron - Houyet
treinwandeling-houyet-gendron.description: Adventurous walk with climbing and scrambling through the forest. Follow the GR126 along impressive rock formations and steep descents to the Lesse.
treinwandeling-houyet-gendron.fullDescription:
This train walk is for adventurers! From Houyet station you follow the GR126 through varied terrain with climbing passages, steep descents and spectacular rock formations.

## How does it work?

You take the train at Gendron station towards Bertrix and get off in Houyet. From Houyet station you walk back to Gendron via the GR126 - the long-distance hiking trail that runs through the Ardennes.

## Character of the walk

This walk is **not suitable for buggies** and requires sturdy footwear. The route includes:

- **Steep stairs** with handrails through the forest
- **Climbing passages** over rock formations
- **Steep descents** where you sometimes need to use your hands
- **Narrow forest paths** with roots and stones

## Highlights along the way

- **Rock formations** - Impressive limestone rocks you can climb on
- **Panoramic views** - Beautiful vistas over the Lesse valley
- **Varied forest** - From dense beech forests to open meadows
- **The Lesse** - The last part runs along the beautiful river

## Practical

Wear sturdy shoes with good grip. In wet weather some sections can be slippery. Bring enough water - there are no facilities along the way.

## Finish in Gendron

The walk ends at Gendron station. From here you can walk back to ArdenNest or take the train back to Dinant for a drink on a terrace.
routeSteps: Take the train from Gendron to Houyet
Leave Houyet station and follow the GR126 signs
Climb down steep stairs into the forest

wandeling-rond-malvoisin.title: Walk around Malvoisin
wandeling-rond-malvoisin.description: Quiet village walk with beautiful panoramic views.
wandeling-rond-malvoisin.fullDescription: A quiet walk from our own village.

laddertjeswandeling-rochehaut.title: Ladder Walk Rochehaut
laddertjeswandeling-rochehaut.description: Spectacular walk with ladders and chains along the rock faces of the Semois. A real adventure hike!
laddertjeswandeling-rochehaut.fullDescription:
The ladder walk of Rochehaut is one of the most spectacular hikes in the Ardennes. You descend along iron ladders and chains fixed to the rock faces, with breathtaking views over the Semois valley.

## What makes it special?

This is no ordinary walk. The route takes you along steep rock faces where iron ladders and chains have been installed to make the descent possible. It's safe but spectacular — perfect for adventure lovers.

## Difficulty

This walk is **not suitable for small children or people with a fear of heights**. You need sturdy hiking boots and the path can be slippery when wet. The ladders require good fitness and a firm grip.

## The view

Rochehaut is known for its breathtaking panorama over the Semois valley. The village itself is one of the most beautiful in Wallonia and also deserves a visit.

## Practical

Depart from the village of Rochehaut. Several route variants are possible. Bring enough water and wear sturdy shoes with good grip.

## From ArdenNest

Rochehaut is about 30 minutes by car. Combine the walk with lunch on a terrace in the village — the view is worth it!

caprando-wandelgids.title: CapRando — Guided Walks
caprando-wandelgids.description: A certified hiking guide who comes to your door. Perfect for companies and families who want a guided walk with an experienced guide who speaks Dutch and French.
caprando-wandelgids.fullDescription:
CapRando is a certified hiking guide who takes you on a discovery tour through the most beautiful landscapes of the Ardennes. Ideal for companies, families or groups of friends who want a guided walk together.

## How does it work?

The guide always meets at the holiday home to get you started. No hassle with meeting at a car park or searching for the starting point — he simply comes to your door.

## Tailored to your group

Every walk is adapted to your level and wishes. From a relaxed nature walk to a sporty day trip of 20 km with elevation. The guide knows the area inside out and plans a route that perfectly suits your group.

## What can you expect?

CapRando offers various formats:

- **Randonnées à la carte** — A full day walk (±20 km) through the Semois valley, the Famenne-Ardenne Geopark or the Great Forest of Saint-Hubert
- **Nature walks** — Shorter walks with attention to flora, fauna and the landscape
- **Orientation initiation** — Learn map reading and navigation in the forest

## The guide

The guide is a certified hiking leader who speaks both Dutch and French. He knows the area like the back of his hand and loves sharing his passion for the Ardennes nature.

## Rates

Day rate: €120 for up to 8 participants (+€10/person extra). Morning coffee included. Minimum 4 participants, maximum 16 for everyone's comfort.

## From ArdenNest

The guide comes directly to the holiday home. Contact well in advance to set a date.

ravel-dinant-givet.title: RAVeL cycle path Dinant - Givet
ravel-dinant-givet.description: Flat cycle path along the Meuse, perfect for families.
ravel-dinant-givet.fullDescription: The RAVeL network offers beautiful, car-free cycle paths along the Meuse.

mountainbike-beauraing.title: Mountain bike trails Beauraing
mountainbike-beauraing.description: Challenging routes through the forests of the Ardennes.
mountainbike-beauraing.fullDescription: For the sporty cyclist there are various mountain bike routes.

la-balade-de-sim.title: La Balade de Sim — Electric bike rental
la-balade-de-sim.description: Electric bikes delivered to your door. Sim brings the bikes to your holiday home and picks them up afterwards — no need to go anywhere.
la-balade-de-sim.fullDescription:
La Balade de Sim is a small, personal bike rental run by a passionate cyclist who knows the region inside out.

## How does it work?

Sim delivers electric bikes directly to your accommodation — at the time of your choice, for the duration you prefer. Afterwards he simply comes to collect them. No driving to a rental point, no queuing.

## The bikes

Quality electric bikes (BH Atom) for a pleasant and effortless cycling experience. Everyone — even those not in top shape — can enjoy the beautiful surroundings.

## The routes

Sim creates personalised routes on request, from Dinant to the Semois, via Houyet and Daverdisse. You can also depart from Castel de Beauraing for one of his 3 fixed routes.

## From ArdenNest

Sim comes right to your door. Book well in advance, especially in high season.
la-balade-de-sim.pricingShortNote: Departure from Castel Saint-Pierre de Beauraing

dinant-evasion.title: Dinant Evasion — Adventure Park
dinant-evasion.description: Spectacular adventure park set in an old quarry above the Meuse. Various courses, death rides and climbing walls.
dinant-evasion.fullDescription:
Dinant Evasion is an adventure park in an old quarry high above the Meuse, just outside Dinant. It's not your average climbing forest — the setting in the raw rock faces makes it more spectacular than any other park in the region.

## What you can do

- 4 ziplines where you fly up to 60 metres above the quarry
- Various challenges: Ardennes Challenge (from 10y), Alps Challenge (from 12y), Mont-Blanc Challenge (from 10y)
- XL Swing, Fun cable and climbing walls
- Paintball and city game in Dinant

## Why we recommend it

The park is open year-round — even off-season. Perfect for teens who need an adrenaline fix. Booking is recommended.

## From ArdenNest

30 minutes by car. Via Dinant, towards the Citadel. The park is set high above the town in an old quarry — the drive there is already an experience.
dinant-evasion.openingHours: Year-round — check the website for exact hours

seo.title: Dinant Evasion — Adventure Park | ArdenNest
seo.description: Spectacular adventure park in an old quarry above the Meuse. Ziplines, climbing walls and challenges for teens. 30 min from ArdenNest.

bomenparcours-han.title: Treetop Course — Caves of Han
bomenparcours-han.description: Unique treetop course in the Wildlife Park of Han. A combination of nature and physical challenge.
bomenparcours-han.fullDescription:
Tree Experience is the brand-new treetop course in the Wildlife Park of Han-sur-Lesse. Not a standard climbing forest, but a well-designed course through the canopy with 5 different levels.

## What you can do

- Courses Everjong & Wolfje: easy, for children from 4 years (max 150 cm)
- Course Beertje: slightly higher, at 2 metres, from 120 cm with supervision
- Courses Uil & Vleermuis: 10 metres high, for real adventurers (from 10 years, min 130 cm) with 140-metre ziplines
- Climbing wall (from 4 years) and free fall (min 130 cm)

## Why we recommend it

Combine the treetop course with a visit to the Wildlife Park or the famous Caves of Han. One ticket, a whole day of fun. Estimated duration treetop course: 1h30. Plan for 11:30 for the best comfort.

## Practical tips

Long hair in ponytail, no open shoes, gloves recommended, max 120kg. Children under 12 accompanied by adult.

## From ArdenNest

20 minutes by car towards Rochefort. Combine with the Caves of Han for a full day.

seo.title: Treetop Course Tree Experience — Caves of Han | ArdenNest
seo.description: 5 courses through the treetops in the Wildlife Park of Han. 140m ziplines, climbing wall and free fall. From age 4. 20 min from ArdenNest.

terraltitude-fumay.title: Terraltitude — Fantasticable
terraltitude-fumay.description: Just across the French border: a zipline at 100 km/h over the Meuse. Pure adrenaline.
terraltitude-fumay.fullDescription:
Just across the French border, in Fumay, lies Terraltitude — the adventure park of the Ardennes. The absolute showstopper: the Fantasticable, a 1.2 km zipline where you fly at over 100 km/h above the Meuse. Not for the faint-hearted.

## What you can do

- **Fantasticable**: 1.2 km zipline over the Meuse at 100+ km/h
- **Accrobranche**: tree climbing course for all levels with suspension bridges, ropes and nets
- **Catapulte**: get catapulted 18 metres into the air by elastic cords — in 2 seconds
- **Tour des Aventuriers**: jump tower for those wanting even more thrills
- **Bois des Nutons**: play area for little ones (2-11 years) with huts, trampolines, karts and barefoot path
- Paintball

## Why we recommend it

This is the only park in the region with an activity of this calibre. The Fantasticable alone is worth the trip. And with the Bois des Nutons, there's plenty for younger children too. Seasonal — usually open from April.

## From ArdenNest

20 minutes by car, just across the French border near Fumay. Bring passport or ID card.
terraltitude-fumay.openingHours: April - November (check the calendar on the website)

seo.title: Terraltitude Fumay — Fantasticable & Adventure Park | ArdenNest
seo.description: Fly at 100 km/h over the Meuse on the Fantasticable. Accrobranche, catapult and play area for little ones. Just across the French border, 20 min from ArdenNest.

cap-nature-bertrix.title: Cap Nature (Bertrix)
cap-nature-bertrix.description: Adventure courses and outdoor sports near the slate quarry. Great mix of sports and nature.
cap-nature-bertrix.fullDescription:
Cap Nature in Bertrix is a climbing forest right next to the Au Coeur de l'Ardoise slate quarry — perfect to combine. The park offers 10 courses for all ages and levels, with a total of 500 metres of ziplines across 12 different lines and 70 different exercises.

## What you can do

- 10 adventure courses from easy to vertigo
- Pom-Pom Adventure for the very youngest
- Monkey bridge, Tibetan bridge, Tarzan jump, acrobatic nets and ziplines
- Safety as priority: annual inspection by Vinçotte

## Why we recommend it

The combination with the slate quarry next door makes for a full day out. Morning underground, afternoon in the trees. Open from Easter holidays.

**Tip:** Combine with [Au Cœur de l'Ardoise](/surroundings/active/leisteengroeve-bertrix) for a full day in Bertrix.

## From ArdenNest

25 minutes by car towards Bertrix.

seo.title: Cap Nature Bertrix — Climbing Forest & Adventure Courses | ArdenNest
seo.description: 10 climbing courses, 500m ziplines and 70 exercises in Bertrix. For all ages. Combine with the slate quarry. 25 min from ArdenNest.

recrealle.title: Récréalle
recrealle.description: Complete recreation area on the Semois with kayak rental, mountain bikes, mini golf and indoor playground.
recrealle.fullDescription:
Récréalle in Alle-sur-Semois is the Swiss army knife of recreation areas. In one place you'll find everything: kayaking on the Semois, mini golf, climbing wall, bowling, paintball, bouncy castle, ice rink, archery and even a swimming zone by the river.

## What you can do

- Kayaking on the Semois (7-11 km routes, €22-€25)
- 11-metre climbing wall (from age 8)
- Bowling, 18-hole mini golf, table tennis
- Bouncy castle + Acro Game for children
- Aqua-bike, Segway, e-bike and mountain bike rental
- Bazooka ball, paintball, archery
- Free swimming zone and playground
- Restaurant, brasserie and BBQ option

## Why we recommend it

The ideal rainy day destination (bowling, climbing wall, snooker indoors) but also perfect in good weather (kayaking, swimming, mini golf). Handy when you have a group with different ages — everyone finds something here.

## From ArdenNest

25 minutes by car towards Vresse-sur-Semois and Alle-sur-Semois.

seo.title: Récréalle — Recreation Area on the Semois | ArdenNest
seo.description: Kayaking, climbing wall, bowling, mini golf, swimming and more in one place on the Semois. Ideal for families with children. 25 min from ArdenNest.

leisteengroeve-bertrix.title: Au Cœur de l'Ardoise — Slate Quarry
leisteengroeve-bertrix.description: Descend into the region's history. Fascinating visit to the old slate quarry deep underground. Always 10°C — perfect on rainy days.
leisteengroeve-bertrix.fullDescription:
Au Cœur de l'Ardoise is an old slate quarry in Bertrix where you descend 25 metres underground into the history of the Ardennes. The mine was active until 1976, and today you walk through gigantic halls blasted from the rock.

## What you can do

- Underground guided tour of 1 hour through nearly 1 km of galleries
- Audio guide in NL, FR and even Walloon (speakers, not headphones, so you can talk to the children)
- Optional: tour with a real former miner (book 10 days ahead, max 25 people)
- Mine Gourmande: visit + 3-course meal with local produce (from 10 people)
- Underground beer tasting (groups from 10 people)
- Combination with Cap Nature climbing forest next door (€32/student for a full day)

## Why we recommend it

Weather-proof — it's always 10°C underground. Perfect on rainy days. Dress warmly. Combine with Cap Nature for a full day: morning underground, afternoon in the trees.

**Note:** The site is currently under renovation. Visits, apéros and meals remain possible. E-Train and e-steps temporarily unavailable.

**Tip:** Combine with [Cap Nature](/surroundings/active/cap-nature-bertrix) for a full day in Bertrix.

## From ArdenNest

25 minutes by car towards Bertrix.
leisteengroeve-bertrix.openingHours: Weekends + school holidays (except Monday). Groups daily by reservation.

seo.title: Au Cœur de l'Ardoise — Slate Quarry Bertrix | ArdenNest
seo.description: Descend 25 metres into an old slate quarry. Always 10°C, ideal on rainy days. Audio guide in NL/FR. Combine with Cap Nature climbing forest. 25 min from ArdenNest.

ardennest-dropping.title: The ArdenNest Dropping
ardennest-dropping.description: A personalised adventure where Christophe maps out a route. Parents drop off the kids — they have to find their way back home. Day edition or Twilight edition (12+).
ardennest-dropping.fullDescription:
The ArdenNest Dropping is one of those experiences you can't book anywhere. Christophe — the owner — maps out a route through the forests and fields around Malvoisin. The parents drop off the kids at a secret location, and together they have to find their way back to the holiday home.

## Two editions

- **Day edition**: during the day, on a scenic route through the surroundings. Suitable for children from 12 years.
- **Twilight edition**: at dusk, for those who want more excitement. Only for 12+.

## How does it work?

Just ask Christophe upon arrival or in advance by email. He adjusts the route to the age and number of participants. Maps, hints and an emergency phone number are provided.

## Why we recommend it

Because it doesn't exist outside ArdenNest. No tickets, no queue, no commercial fuss. Just a tailor-made adventure in your own temporary backyard.

seo.title: The ArdenNest Dropping — Exclusive for Guests | ArdenNest
seo.description: A tailor-made dropping through the forests of Malvoisin. Day or twilight edition. Exclusively for ArdenNest guests. From age 12.

dinant.title: Dinant
dinant.description: Picturesque town on the Meuse with citadel and collegiate church.
dinant.fullDescription: Dinant is the nearest town and offers a perfect day out.

grotten-van-han.title: Caves of Han
grotten-van-han.fullDescription: A must-see for every visit to the Ardennes.

kasteel-bouillon.title: Bouillon Castle
kasteel-bouillon.description: Medieval castle with birds of prey show.
kasteel-bouillon.fullDescription: Step back in time in this imposing medieval castle.

abdij-maredsous.title: Maredsous Abbey
abdij-maredsous.description: Benedictine abbey with famous cheese and beers.
abdij-maredsous.fullDescription: An oasis of peace with delicious regional products.

le-belvedere-beauraing.title: Le Belvédère
le-belvedere-beauraing.description: Our favourite spot in Beauraing. Half café, half restaurant, entirely itself. The kind of place where everyone belongs.
le-belvedere-beauraing.fullDescription:
There are places you walk into and immediately think: I'll be back. Le Belvédère in Beauraing is one of those places.

## The atmosphere

It's hard to pin down what Le Belvédère actually is. A café? A restaurant? It's both, and neither. Everyone gathers around the bar — and that's what makes it beautiful. You'll stand next to a biker fresh off a ride, a couple of day-trippers, a worker still dusty from the job, a sales rep between appointments. Nobody bats an eye. Everyone belongs.

## The hostess

The owner is what makes this place tick. Warm, charming, and genuinely attentive to whoever walks in. The kind of hostess who puts you at ease — even on your very first visit.

## What you eat

No endless menu with impossible dishes. Just fresh, honest food, every time. The weekly special is reliably excellent. The kind of meal that leaves you quietly happy.

## Coming with a large group?

Call well in advance. They'll gladly think along and make sure everything runs smoothly — without it ever feeling stiff or formal. Exactly as it should be.

## From the holiday home

A fifteen-minute drive from ArdenNest. Perfect for an evening where nobody has to cook and everyone can simply enjoy.

hotel-de-la-poste-gedinne.title: Hôtel de la Poste
hotel-de-la-poste-gedinne.description: The village restaurant of Gedinne. Brasserie, grill and restaurant in one — where locals and holidaymakers share the same tables.
hotel-de-la-poste-gedinne.fullDescription:
Some restaurants you don't need to look for. They're simply there, right in the heart of the village, for as long as anyone can remember. Hôtel de la Poste in Gedinne is one of those places.

## The atmosphere

This is where Gedinne eats. Locals dropping in for a steak after work, families with their usual Sunday table, holidaymakers who have a house in the area and found their second home here. The owner also runs café Le Notaire — right next door — and that combination says it all: this is the beating heart of the village.

## The host

The patron knows everyone — and everyone knows him. You're welcomed the way it should be in the Ardennes: no fuss, just genuine warmth. The kind of place where they remember what you had last time and greet you by name after just two visits.

## What you eat

Brasserie and grill — honest and generous. Fresh dishes, seasonal suggestions, and grilled meat that is exactly what you'd hope for: good. No culinary acrobatics, just food that leaves you properly satisfied.

## Coming with a large group?

Enquire well in advance about the options and how many guests they can accommodate. For a large group, booking is a must — they're happy to arrange it, but need to be able to count on it.

## From the holiday home

Just five minutes by car. The nearest restaurant to ArdenNest — ideal for an evening without cooking stress.

le-matefaim-graide-station.title: Le Matefaim
le-matefaim-graide-station.description: Family-run restaurant-grill in Graide-Station. Father behind the stove, mother in the dining room. Everything homemade, honest and generous.
le-matefaim-graide-station.fullDescription:
Some restaurants endure simply because they do exactly what they should — and have done so for years. Le Matefaim in Graide-Station is one of those places.

## The atmosphere

This is a restaurant where you'll see plenty of locals. Older couples who've had their regular table here for years, families from the area, holidaymakers who stumbled upon it once and have come back every year since. That mix says enough: everyone feels at home here.

## The hostess

Mother runs the dining room with infectious energy. Always busy, always warm. The kind of hostess who makes you feel welcome — not as a customer, but as a guest.

## What you eat

Father is behind the stove, and you can taste it. No endless menu, but everything homemade. Grilled meats on lava stone, regional cuisine, game in season. The daily specials are chalked up on the blackboard and are reliably worth ordering. The kind of honest food you come back for.

## Coming with a large group?

They've proven in the past that they handle larger groups perfectly — up to 40 guests in the dining room. Book well in advance and they'll gladly arrange it.

## From the holiday home

About ten minutes by car. A no-fuss evening out where everyone leaves the table satisfied.

la-saliere-gedinne.title: La Salière
la-saliere-gedinne.description: Wood-fired pizzas with a thin, crispy crust. The go-to takeaway for groups wanting pizza at the holiday home.
la-saliere-gedinne.fullDescription:
La Salière in Gedinne is the address when you want to order pizzas for a large group. Thin crust, wood-fired, honestly delicious — it doesn't need to be more than that.

## The atmosphere

A cozy place in the center of Gedinne, popular with both local families and holidaymakers. There's plenty of room to eat in with a larger group, but among our guests La Salière is especially loved as a takeaway address. Order pizzas, bring them back to the holiday home, and everyone's happy.

## What you eat

Pizzas baked in a real wood-fired oven, with a thin, crispy crust. The menu is straightforward — classics that are simply good. No fuss, just flavour. Exactly the kind of pizza a group of 20 can agree on without discussion.

## Coming with a large group?

Absolutely. They're used to large orders. Our tip: order just before opening time for the shortest wait for takeaway. That way, the boxes are ready when you need them.

## From the holiday home

5 minutes drive in the center of Gedinne. Order, drive over, and within half an hour everyone's sitting at the table.

le-petit-creux-by-roselle-gedinne.title: Le Petit Creux By Roselle
le-petit-creux-by-roselle-gedinne.description: Fresh frites shop in the heart of Gedinne. Everything prepared with care by an owner who's meticulous in her craft. Perfect for takeaway.
le-petit-creux-by-roselle-gedinne.fullDescription:
Sometimes you just want a portion of fresh fries. Le Petit Creux By Roselle in Gedinne delivers exactly that — and more.

## The atmosphere

This is a proper village chip shop, just as they should be. Small, cozy, and run by someone who puts her heart into it. Not the place to sit down with a large group — there simply isn't enough room. But for takeaway, it's perfect.

## The owner

Roselle is meticulous in her finishing. Every portion is prepared with care, nothing is carelessly pulled from the fryer. You can taste it. Everything is fresh, honest, and made with attention.

## What you eat

Fresh fries, snacks, and — if you're truly hungry — order a mitraillette. The Walloon specialty: a baguette stuffed with meat, fries and sauce. All the young people in the villages eat this. We recommend it if you want to taste what locals eat.

## Please note

The shop isn't always open. Check the opening hours in advance or give them a call.

## From the holiday home

5 minutes drive in Gedinne. Order, take away, and serve at the table in the holiday home.

friterie-de-la-ferme-bievre.title: Friterie de la Ferme
friterie-de-la-ferme-bievre.description: A chip shop on a working farm in Bièvre. Order with a pen on a menu card, grab your own drinks from the fridge, and take a stroll around the farm while you wait.
friterie-de-la-ferme-bievre.fullDescription:
There are chip shops, and then there's Friterie de la Ferme. This isn't your ordinary frites place — it's an experience.

## The atmosphere

You drive onto a farm and there it is: the chip shop, right in the middle of barns and pastures. It's immediately clear you're not in a standard establishment. The vibe is relaxed, authentic, and a little quirky — in the best possible way.

## Their own approach

Things work differently here. You get a menu card and a pen, and you tick off what you'd like to eat. Drinks? Grab them yourself from the fridge. No waiters, no pretence, just: here it is, help yourself. And it works surprisingly well.

## What you eat

Everything is freshly prepared. Fresh fries, snacks, and the classics you'd expect from a good chip shop. Generous portions, honest flavour.

## Coming with a large group?

Absolutely — there's plenty of room to eat with a group here. That's the big difference with many other chip shops in the area. Enough tables, enough space, and the atmosphere only gets better for it.

## The farm stroll

While you wait for your order, take a little walk around the farm. Children love it, adults enjoy the peace. It's an outing in itself.

## From the holiday home

About ten minutes drive towards Bièvre. A chip shop evening with a twist — the kids will be talking about it for days.

boulangerie-dumont.title: Boulangerie Dumont
boulangerie-dumont.description: Artisan bakery in Vencimont with fresh bread, pastries and cakes. Online ordering available with 3% discount.
boulangerie-dumont.fullDescription:
Boulangerie Dumont in Vencimont is a genuine artisan bakery where you can find delicious fresh bread, pastries and celebration cakes.

## What can you expect?

This family bakery offers an extensive range:

- **Boulangerie** - Fresh bread in all varieties, from classic rolls to rustic country loaves
- **Pâtisserie** - Fine pastries and tarts, perfect with coffee
- **Gâteaux** - Beautiful cakes for birthdays and celebrations
- **Seasonal products** - Special items for holidays

## Online ordering

Convenient: you can place your order online via their website and receive 3% discount. Order today, collect tomorrow!

## Bread vending machine

Also handy: there's a bread vending machine with fresh bread, available on Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday, Saturday and Sunday.

## Collection points

Can't make it to Vencimont? You can also collect your order at:
- Proxy Delhaize Pondrôme
- Total Station Pondrôme

## Opening hours

The shop is closed on Monday, Tuesday and Thursday. Other days the bakery is open and fresh bread is placed in the vending machine.

## From ArdenNest

The bakery is in Vencimont, about 10 minutes drive. Perfect to combine with a visit to the butcher Aux Délices de la Houille in the same village!
boulangerie-dumont.openingHours: Wed, Fri, Sat, Sun: 7am-1pm · Closed: Mon, Tue, Thu

proxy-delhaize-gedinne.title: Proxy Delhaize Gedinne
proxy-delhaize-gedinne.description: Neighborhood store with extensive range, open 7 days a week.
proxy-delhaize-gedinne.fullDescription:
The Proxy Delhaize in Gedinne is our nearest supermarket and has everything you need for your stay.

## What can you expect?

This neighborhood store combines supermarket convenience with personal village shop service. You'll find:

- **Fresh bread and pastries** - Fresh from the oven daily
- **Vegetables and fruit** - Wide choice of fresh products
- **Meat and charcuterie** - Including local specialties
- **Drinks** - Belgian beers, wine and soft drinks
- **Daily groceries** - Everything for breakfast, lunch and dinner

## Opening hours

The shop is open 7 days a week, including Sunday! Ideal if you've forgotten something or want a spontaneous barbecue.

## From ArdenNest

The Proxy Delhaize is just 5 minutes drive in the center of Gedinne. Easy to combine with other shopping in the village.

intermarche-gedinne.title: Intermarché Gedinne
intermarche-gedinne.description: Modern supermarket with excellent fresh section, bakery and butcher.
intermarche-gedinne.fullDescription:
Intermarché Gedinne is a spacious, modern supermarket with everything you need for your stay.

## What makes this store special?

Intermarché is known for their excellent fresh departments:

- **La Boulangerie** - Freshly baked bread and pastries, from baguettes to croissants
- **La Rotisserie** - Prepared dishes and grilled meats, perfect for an easy meal
- **Le Traiteur** - Salads and cold dishes
- **Butcher** - Quality meat and charcuterie
- **Fruit and vegetables** - Wide selection, partly from local producers

## Practical

The store has ample parking and is easily accessible. Ideal for a big shop on arrival or to restock supplies.

## From ArdenNest

Intermarché is 5 minutes drive on the edge of Gedinne. Perfect to combine with a visit to the village.

aux-delices-de-la-houille.title: Aux Délices de la Houille
aux-delices-de-la-houille.description: Artisan butcher that processes, smokes and cures everything themselves. Family business since 1972.
aux-delices-de-la-houille.fullDescription:
Aux Délices de la Houille in Vencimont is no ordinary butcher. This family business has been making artisan charcuterie since 1972 and specializes in salaisons - smoked and cured meats.

## What makes this butcher special?

Here they only sell what they make themselves. From farm to counter, they control every step of the process:

- **House-made salaisons** - Smoked and cured specialties, the pride of the house
- **Artisan charcuterie** - Pâtés, terrines and sausages following traditional recipes
- **Fresh meat** - Quality meat from local farms
- **Seasonal products** - BBQ meats in summer, game in autumn

## The passion of a craftsman

As soon as you enter, you see the difference from an ordinary butcher. The hanging sausages, the smoked hams, the carefully prepared products - here you taste craftsmanship.

## Opening hours

Open Wednesday to Saturday, 9am-12pm and 2pm-6pm. Closed Sunday, Monday and Tuesday.

## From ArdenNest

The butcher is located in Vencimont, about 10 minutes drive. A visit is worth the detour, especially if you love quality meat and artisan charcuterie.

terracines.title: Terracines
terracines.description: Organic vegetable garden with farm shop in Beauraing. Fresh seasonal vegetables straight from the field to your plate.
terracines.fullDescription:
Terracines in Beauraing is a beautiful organic vegetable garden where you can buy the freshest vegetables straight from the field.

## From seed to plate

Their motto says it all: 'De la graine à votre assiette' - from seed to your plate. Everything grows with care and attention, without pesticides, in harmony with the seasons.

## What can you expect?

The selection changes with the seasons:

- **Spring** - First lettuce, radishes, spinach and fresh herbs
- **Summer** - Tomatoes in all colors, zucchini, beans, peppers
- **Autumn** - Pumpkins, leeks, cabbages, carrots
- **Winter** - Winter vegetables and storage crops

## Three ways to buy

- **Saturday (9:30am-1:30pm)** - The big market with beautiful displays
- **Wednesday (10am-4pm)** - Bulk & freshness: pick straight from harvest crates
- **Thursday/Friday (9am-7pm)** - Collection by order

## Online ordering

You can also place your order online via their website. Handy if you want to be sure of your favorite vegetables!

## Note: winter break

The garden rests in winter (closed until April). Check the website for current opening times.

## From ArdenNest

Terracines is located in Beauraing, about 15 minutes drive. A visit is worth it for anyone who loves honest, fresh vegetables!
terracines.openingHours: Sat: 9:30am-1:30pm · Wed: 10am-4pm · Thu-Fri: by order · Winter break
